---
title: "Research"
description: "Publications, conference contributions, and professional involvement"
toc: true
---

```{=html}
<style>
.pub-entry { margin-bottom: 1.6rem; padding-bottom: 1.4rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #e9ecef; }
.pub-year  { font-weight: 700; color: #1a5276; font-size: 0.85rem; }
.pub-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; color: #1a2b3c; display: block; margin: 4px 0 2px; }
.pub-journal { font-size: 0.88rem; color: #555; display: block; margin-bottom: 6px; }
.pub-note  { font-size: 0.83rem; color: #6c757d; font-style: italic; display: block; margin-bottom: 8px; line-height: 1.5; }
.pub-tags  { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 5px; }
.pub-tag   {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #eaf3fb; color: #1a5276;
  font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600;
  padding: 2px 9px; border-radius: 10px;
}
</style>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2024</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Probing liver injuries induced by thioacetamide in human <em>in vitro</em> pooled hepatocyte experiments</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Printz RL, Pannala VR, AbdulHameed MDM, Wallqvist A. <em>Int. J. Mol. Sci.</em> 2024, 25, 3265</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Identified transcriptomic biomarkers of drug-induced liver injury (DILI) using RNA-Seq on human pooled hepatocytes exposed to thioacetamide — supporting safer chemical risk assessment.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">RNA-Seq</span><span class="pub-tag">DILI</span><span class="pub-tag">Hepatotoxicity</span><span class="pub-tag">Biomarkers</span><span class="pub-tag">Toxicogenomics</span><span class="pub-tag">Kallisto</span><span class="pub-tag">Human Hepatocytes</span><span class="pub-tag">Transcriptomics</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2024</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Modeling Ligand Binding Site Water Networks with SILCS: Impact on Ligand Binding Orientations and Relative Binding Affinities</span>
  <span class="pub-journal">Kumar A, <strong>Goel H</strong>, Yu W, Zhao M, MacKerell Jr. AD. <em>J. Chem. Theory Comput.</em> 2024, 20, 11032–11048</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Demonstrated that explicitly modeling water networks in protein binding pockets with SILCS-MC Water significantly improves prediction of drug binding pose and affinity — key for rational drug design.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">Water Networks</span><span class="pub-tag">Binding Affinity</span><span class="pub-tag">SILCS</span><span class="pub-tag">Drug Design</span><span class="pub-tag">MD Simulations</span><span class="pub-tag">Binding Pose</span><span class="pub-tag">Protein–Ligand</span><span class="pub-tag">Solvation</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2023</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Assessing kidney injury induced by mercuric chloride in guinea pigs with <em>in vivo</em> and <em>in vitro</em> experiments</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Printz RL, Shiota C, Estes SK, Pannala VR, AbdulHameed MDM, Shiota M, Wallqvist A. <em>Int. J. Mol. Sci.</em> 2023, 24, 7434</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Cross-species toxicogenomics study bridging in vivo guinea pig and in vitro models to identify kidney injury biomarkers — advancing translational safety assessment for heavy metal toxicity.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">Nephrotoxicity</span><span class="pub-tag">RNA-Seq</span><span class="pub-tag">Cross-Species</span><span class="pub-tag">IVIVE</span><span class="pub-tag">Heavy Metals</span><span class="pub-tag">Guinea Pig</span><span class="pub-tag">DEG Analysis</span><span class="pub-tag">Renal Biomarkers</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2022</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Application of SILCS in Computer-Aided Drug Design</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Hazel A, Yu W, Jo S, MacKerell Jr. AD. <em>New J. Chem.</em> 2022, 46, 919–932</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Comprehensive review and demonstration of the SILCS CADD workflow — from FragMaps to pharmacophore modeling and virtual screening — making the technology accessible for the broader drug discovery community.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">CADD</span><span class="pub-tag">Virtual Screening</span><span class="pub-tag">Pharmacophore</span><span class="pub-tag">SILCS</span><span class="pub-tag">FragMaps</span><span class="pub-tag">Hit Identification</span><span class="pub-tag">SAR</span><span class="pub-tag">Hotspot Analysis</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2022</span>
  <span class="pub-title">hERG Blockade prediction by combining SILCS and Physicochemical Properties</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Yu W, MacKerell Jr. AD. <em>Chemistry</em> 2022, 4, 630–646</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Combined 3D molecular interaction maps (SILCS) with physicochemical descriptors to predict hERG1 cardiac ion channel blockade — a critical early-stage safety filter that flags cardiotoxic compounds before costly experiments.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">hERG</span><span class="pub-tag">Cardiotoxicity</span><span class="pub-tag">Safety Pharmacology</span><span class="pub-tag">ML</span><span class="pub-tag">SILCS</span><span class="pub-tag">Ion Channel</span><span class="pub-tag">Physicochemical</span><span class="pub-tag">Drug Safety</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2021</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Rapid and Accurate Estimation of Protein–Ligand Relative Binding Affinities using SILCS</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Hazel A, Ustach V, Jo S, Yu W, MacKerell Jr. AD. <em>Chem. Sci.</em> 2021, 12, 8844–8858</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Validated SILCS as a fast, accurate alternative to computationally expensive FEP for ranking drug candidates by binding affinity — enabling higher-throughput lead optimization.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">Binding Affinity</span><span class="pub-tag">FEP</span><span class="pub-tag">Lead Optimization</span><span class="pub-tag">SILCS</span><span class="pub-tag">Virtual Screening</span><span class="pub-tag">Free Energy</span><span class="pub-tag">Drug Ranking</span><span class="pub-tag">CADD</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2020</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Impact of Electronic Polarizability on Protein Functional Group Interactions</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Yu W, Ustach V, Aytenfisu AH, Sun D, MacKerell Jr. AD. <em>Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys.</em> 2020, 22, 6848</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Quantified how including electronic polarizability (Drude force field) changes affinity patterns for protein functional groups — informing more physically accurate force field design for drug discovery.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">Polarizable Force Field</span><span class="pub-tag">Drude</span><span class="pub-tag">Protein Interactions</span><span class="pub-tag">CHARMM</span><span class="pub-tag">SILCS</span><span class="pub-tag">Electronic Polarizability</span><span class="pub-tag">Functional Groups</span><span class="pub-tag">Force Field</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2019</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Absorption of CO₂ in Triethyl(butyl)phosphonium 2-Cyanopyrrolide Ionic Liquid via First Principles Simulations</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Windom Z, Jackson A, Rai N. <em>J. Mol. Liq.</em> 2019, 292, 111323</span>
  <span class="pub-note">First-principles prediction of CO₂ capture capacity and structural mechanisms in a phosphonium ionic liquid — relevant to designing next-generation carbon capture solvents.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">CO₂ Capture</span><span class="pub-tag">Ionic Liquids</span><span class="pub-tag">DFT</span><span class="pub-tag">Carbon Capture</span><span class="pub-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="pub-tag">Phosphonium</span><span class="pub-tag">Adsorption</span><span class="pub-tag">Green Chemistry</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2018</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Predicting Vapor Liquid Equilibria using Density Functional Theory: A Case Study of Argon</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Ling S, Ellis BN, Taconi A, Slater B, Rai N. <em>J. Chem. Phys.</em> 2018, 148, 224501</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Established a rigorous benchmark for DFT-based VLE prediction using argon as a model system, guiding selection of exchange-correlation functionals for condensed-phase property prediction.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">DFT</span><span class="pub-tag">VLE</span><span class="pub-tag">Benchmark</span><span class="pub-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="pub-tag">CP2K</span><span class="pub-tag">Exchange-Correlation</span><span class="pub-tag">Condensed Phase</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2018</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Transferable Potentials for Chloroethenes: Insights into Nonideal Solution Behavior of Environmental Contaminants</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Rai N. <em>ACS Omega</em> 2018, 3, 3646–3654</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Developed TraPPE force fields for chlorinated ethenes and explained nonideal mixing behavior — directly applicable to modeling the fate and transport of these common groundwater contaminants.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">Force Field</span><span class="pub-tag">TraPPE</span><span class="pub-tag">Environmental Contaminants</span><span class="pub-tag">Chloroethenes</span><span class="pub-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="pub-tag">Groundwater</span><span class="pub-tag">Partition Coefficient</span><span class="pub-tag">Solvation</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2018</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Solubility of CO₂ in Triglycerides using Monte Carlo Simulations</span>
  <span class="pub-journal">Howlader MS, Venkatesan S, <strong>Goel H</strong>, French WT, Rai N. <em>Fluid Phase Equilib.</em> 2018, 476, 39–47</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Predicted CO₂ solubility in triglycerides via Monte Carlo — important for supercritical CO₂ extraction processes in biodiesel and food processing applications.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">CO₂ Solubility</span><span class="pub-tag">Triglycerides</span><span class="pub-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="pub-tag">Biofuels</span><span class="pub-tag">Phase Equilibria</span><span class="pub-tag">Supercritical CO₂</span><span class="pub-tag">Extraction</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2018</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Performance of Density Functionals for Modeling Vapor Liquid Equilibria of CO₂ and SO₂</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Jackson A, Windom Z, Rai N. <em>J. Comp. Chem.</em> 2018</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Benchmarked a range of DFT exchange-correlation functionals for predicting phase behavior of acid gases — providing practical guidance for first-principles simulation of carbon capture systems.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">DFT</span><span class="pub-tag">VLE</span><span class="pub-tag">CO₂</span><span class="pub-tag">SO₂</span><span class="pub-tag">Carbon Capture</span><span class="pub-tag">Acid Gases</span><span class="pub-tag">Exchange-Correlation</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2017</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Phase Equilibria and Condensed Phase Properties for Fluorinated Alkanes via First Principles Simulations</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Butler C, Windom Z, Rai N. <em>Chemistry Select</em> 2017, 2, 11969–11976</span>
  <span class="pub-note">First-principles thermodynamic properties for hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) — supporting design and selection of low-global-warming-potential refrigerants as alternatives to older HFCs.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">HFCs</span><span class="pub-tag">Refrigerants</span><span class="pub-tag">DFT</span><span class="pub-tag">Thermodynamics</span><span class="pub-tag">Phase Equilibria</span><span class="pub-tag">Fluorinated Alkanes</span><span class="pub-tag">Climate</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2016</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Vapor Liquid Equilibria of Hydrofluorocarbons Using Dispersion-Corrected and Nonlocal Density Functionals</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Butler C, Windom Z, Rai N. <em>J. Chem. Theory Comput.</em> 2016, 12, 3295–3304</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Showed that dispersion-corrected DFT significantly improves VLE predictions for HFCs — an important methodological advance for accurately modeling fluorinated refrigerant properties from first principles.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">HFCs</span><span class="pub-tag">DFT-D</span><span class="pub-tag">Dispersion Correction</span><span class="pub-tag">VLE</span><span class="pub-tag">Refrigerants</span><span class="pub-tag">Nonlocal Functionals</span><span class="pub-tag">First Principles</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-entry">
  <span class="pub-year">2014</span>
  <span class="pub-title">Estimation of Interfacial Tension for Immiscible and Partially Miscible Liquid Systems by Dissipative Particle Dynamics</span>
  <span class="pub-journal"><strong>Goel H</strong>, Chandran R, Mitra K, Majumdar S, Ray P. <em>Chem. Phys. Lett.</em> 2014, 600, 62–67</span>
  <span class="pub-note">Applied dissipative particle dynamics (DPD) to predict interfacial tension for immiscible and partially miscible liquid systems — relevant to emulsion stability, liquid–liquid extraction, and chemical process design.</span>
  <div class="pub-tags">
    <span class="pub-tag">DPD</span><span class="pub-tag">Interfacial Tension</span><span class="pub-tag">Liquid Mixtures</span><span class="pub-tag">Mesoscale Simulation</span><span class="pub-tag">Emulsions</span><span class="pub-tag">Liquid–Liquid Extraction</span><span class="pub-tag">Thermodynamics</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Publications

### Talks
- **AIChE 2014**, Atlanta — *Force Field Development of Chloroethenes*
- **AIChE 2014**, Atlanta — *VLE for Hydrofluorocarbons via First Principle Monte Carlo*
- **AIChE 2015**, Salt Lake City — *VLE of Hydrofluoromethanes via First Principles Monte Carlo*
- **AIChE 2016**, San Francisco — *Bulk Phase and Structural Properties for CO₂ and SO₂ via First Principles*
- **APS Meeting 2018**, New Orleans — *Accuracy of DFT to Predict Vapor Liquid Equilibria*

### Posters
- **AIChE 2014**, Atlanta — *Octanol Partition Coefficient for Trichloroethylene via Molecular Simulations*
- **AIChE 2015**, Salt Lake City — *Force Field Development of Chloroethenes*
- **AIChE 2016**, San Francisco — *Predicting Octanol-Water Partition Coefficient and Force Field Development for NDMA*
- **AIChE 2016**, San Francisco — *Modeling Sorption and Phase Equilibria using CP2K Software Suite*
- **CCTCC 2017**, Jackson, MS — *Modeling Phase and Sorption Equilibria using CP2K Software Suite*
- **BPS 2019** — *Comparison of Functional Group Affinity Patterns from Additive vs Drude Polarizable Force Fields (SILCS)*
- **BPS 2021** — *Capturing Water-mediated Interactions During Protein–Ligand Binding with SILCS*
- **SOT 2023** — *Assessing kidney injury induced by mercuric chloride in guinea pigs*
- **SOT 2024** — *Probing liver injuries induced by thioacetamide in human in vitro hepatocytes*
- **Poster Chair**, Computational Toxicology I, SOT 2024

---

## Professional involvement

**Memberships:** American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE) · Biophysical Society (BPS) · Society of Toxicology (SOT)

**Peer reviewer** for: *ACS Med. Chem. Lett.*, *J. Chem. Inf. Model.*, *J. Phys. Chem. Lett.*, *Chem. Sci.*, *J. Chem. Eng. Data*, *RSC Med. Chem.*, *New J. Chem.*, *Mach. Learn.: Sci. Technol.*, *Front. Genet.*, *Molecules*, *Int. J. Mol. Sci.*, *New J. Phys.*, *BBA–General Subjects*, *ChemEngineering*, *Materials*, *Processes*, *Applied Science*, *Metals*, *Catalysts*, *Symmetry*, *Computation*
